I found the ticket detail UI in `src/renderer/src/App.tsx` and the main storage/IPC handlers in `src/main/index.ts` plus `src/main/services/storage.ts`. Next I’m reading those narrow sections to preserve the current save/run behavior.

{
  "name": "relay",

The app already has a Codex event/log pipeline for implementation runs and a separate synchronous draft-generation flow. I’m going to add a third path: a read-only ticket-edit agent run that emits the same run events but only applies a validated title/priority/labels/markdown/clarification update, leaving implementation-run metadata alone.
I’m starting with the shared contract so the renderer, preload bridge, and main service agree on a narrow response shape before any UI can call it.

The main service now has the read-only update run skeleton: it starts a separate active run map, streams normal agent events, parses a strict update object on completion, and applies only allowed fields. Next I’m exposing that through IPC and then I’ll hook the detail panel to it.

}

The detail panel now has the new input, submit/stop/log controls, and a separate progress summary that listens only to the ticket-update run. I’m adding the styling next so it fits the existing panel layout without mixing into implementation-run activity.
The core code and tests are in place. I’m running the project checks now; TypeScript will likely catch any mismatch between the Codex SDK stream types and the test doubles.

**Summary**
Implemented agent-assisted ticket updates from the ticket detail panel. The new workflow runs Codex in read-only mode, asks for a strict structured ticket update, validates it, applies only allowed fields, persists through existing `.relay` ticket storage, and leaves implementation-run metadata alone. The UI now shows update progress/logs, prevents duplicate active submissions, supports cancellation, clears the request on success, and shows recoverable errors on failure or invalid output.

payload.finalResponse:
**Summary**
Implemented agent-assisted ticket updates from the ticket detail panel. The new workflow runs Codex in read-only mode, asks for a strict structured ticket update, validates it, applies only allowed fields, persists through existing `.relay` ticket storage, and leaves implementation-run metadata alone. The UI now shows update progress/logs, prevents duplicate active submissions, supports cancellation, clears the request on success, and shows recoverable errors on failure or invalid output.

**Files Changed**
- [src/shared/types.ts](/Users/robertpitt/Projects/relay/src/shared/types.ts)
- [src/main/services/schemas.ts](/Users/robertpitt/Projects/relay/src/main/services/schemas.ts)
- [src/main/services/codex.ts](/Users/robertpitt/Projects/relay/src/main/services/codex.ts)

- [src/renderer/src/App.tsx](/Users/robertpitt/Projects/relay/src/renderer/src/App.tsx)
- [src/renderer/src/styles.css](/Users/robertpitt/Projects/relay/src/renderer/src/styles.css)
- [tests/ticket-update.test.ts](/Users/robertpitt/Projects/relay/tests/ticket-update.test.ts)
- [tests/run-tests.mjs](/Users/robertpitt/Projects/relay/tests/run-tests.mjs)

**Commands Run**

- Verification: `npm run typecheck`, `npm test`, `npm run build`
- Cleanup checks: `git diff --check`, `git status --short`

- `npm test`: passed, 45/45 tests

**Remaining Risks**
